verdict.answer_first: A gets to the actionable fix fastest: "Store a salted hash... and verify against it" appears immediately after the first sentence; B also answers early but adds more setup before the final bottom line.
verdict.decisiveness: B is more forceful: "Don't ship this. The fix is: hash passwords at signup, verify with the hash library at login" commits clearly.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it shows both signup hashing and login verification with a null guard: "At signup... const hash" and "At login: if (user && await argon2.verify(...))".
